steps.label: What we want to prove
steps.explanation: Negation in natural deduction is defined as: ¬P means P → ⊥ (P implies absurdity). To prove ¬P, you assume P and derive a contradiction (⊥). This is proof by contradiction — or more precisely, negation introduction.

steps.label: Assume P, derive ⊥
steps.explanation: ¬-Introduction says: if assuming P leads to absurdity (⊥), then ¬P. We assume P (bracketed, to be discharged), combine it with our premise P → ⊥ using →-Elim to get ⊥, and then discharge the assumption.
